title: Rate conversion
description: Convert a given rate to an equivalent rate in different units. For example, convert 10 km/h to 36 m/s.

assessment_items.question_content:
In $2016$, the city of Rio de Janeiro had a population density of $5377\,\dfrac{\text{people}}{\text{km}^2}$ .

**What was the population density of Rio de Janeiro in people per square meter?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\dfrac{\text{people}}{\text{m}^2}$
hints:
We will convert $5377$ people per square kilometer to a rate in people per square meter using the following conversion rate:

- There are $1000\text{ m}$ per $1\text{ km}$. Therefore, there are $(1000\text{ m})^2=1{,}000{,}000\text{ m}^2$ in $1\text{ km}^2$.

In conclusion, Rio de Janeiro's population density in people per square meter is:

$0.005377 \,\dfrac{\text{people}}{\text{m}^2}$

assessment_items.question_content:
The batting Wang Xiu Ying uses to fill quilts has a thermal conductivity rate of $0.03$ watts $(\text{W})$ per meter $(\text{m})$ per degree Celsius $(^\circ\text{C})$.

**What is the batting's thermal conductivity value in $\dfrac{\text{W}}{\text{cm}\cdot^\circ\text{C}}$?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\dfrac{\text{W}}{\text{cm}\cdot^\circ\text{C}}$
hints:
We will convert $0.03\,\dfrac{\text{W}}{\text{m}\cdot^\circ\text{C}}$ to a thermal conductivity rate in $\dfrac{\text{W}}{\text{cm}\cdot^\circ\text{C}}$ using the following conversion rate:

- There are $100\text{ cm}$ per $1\text{ m}$.

In conclusion, the thermal conductivity rate in $\dfrac{\text{W}}{\text{cm}\cdot^\circ\text{C}}$ is:

$0.0003\,\dfrac{\text{W}}{\text{cm}\cdot^\circ\text{C}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
The water pressure on Mustafa as he dives is increasing at a rate of $0.992$ atmospheres $(\text{atm})$ per meter $(\text{m})$ .

**What is the rate of increase in water pressure in $\dfrac{\text{atm}}{\text{km}}$?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\dfrac{\text{atm}}{\text{km}}$
hints:
We will convert $0.992\,\dfrac{\text{atm}}{\text{m}}$ to a rate in $\dfrac{\text{atm}}{\text{km}}$ using the following conversion rate:

- There are $1000\text{ m}$ per $1\text{ km}$.

In conclusion, the rate of increase in water pressure in $\dfrac{\text{atm}}{\text{km}}$ is:

$992\,\dfrac{\text{atm}}{\text{km}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
An electrolyte solution has an average current density of $1$ ampere per square decimeter $\left( \dfrac{\text{A}}{\text{dm}^2}\right)$.

**What is the current density of the solution in $\dfrac{\text{A}}{\text{m}^2}$?**

[https://www.comsol.com/offers/conference2012papers/papers/file/id/13293/file/15239_lima_paper.pdf]: (Source)

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\dfrac{\text{A}}{\text{m}^2}$
hints:
We will convert $1\, \dfrac{\text{A}}{\text{dm}^2}$ to a rate in $\dfrac{\text{A}}{\text{m}^2}$ using the following conversion rate:

- There are $10\text{ dm}$ per $1\text{ m}$. Therefore, there are $(10\text{ dm})^2=100\text{ dm}^2$ per $1\text{ m}^2$.

In conclusion, the current density of the solution in $\dfrac{\text{A}}{\text{m}^2}$ is:

$100\,\dfrac{\text{A}}{\text{m}^2}$

assessment_items.question_content:
A pitcher's arm rotates at a speed of $7$ degrees per millisecond $\left( \dfrac{\text{degrees}}{\text{ms}} \right)$. 

**At what speed does the pitcher's arm rotate in $\dfrac{\text{degrees}}{\text{s}}$?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\dfrac{\text{degrees}}{\text{s}}$
hints:
We will convert $7\,\dfrac{\text{degrees}}{\text{ms}}$ to a rate in $\dfrac{\text{degrees}}{\text{s}}$ using the following conversion rate:

- There are $1000\text{ ms}$ per $1\text{ s}$.

In conclusion, the acceleration rate in $\dfrac{\text{degrees}}{\text{s}}$ is:

$7000\,\dfrac{\text{degrees}}{\text{s}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Charlotte is running at a rate of $9\,\dfrac{\text{km}}{\text{h}}$.

**What is Charlotte's speed in $\dfrac{\text{m}}{\text{s}}$?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\dfrac{\text{m}}{\text{s}}$
hints:
We will convert $9\,\dfrac{\text{km}}{\text{h}}$ to a rate in $\dfrac{\text{m}}{\text{s}}$ using the following conversion rates:

- There are $1000\text{ m}$ per $1\text{ km}$.
- There is $1\text{ h}$ per $3600\text{ s}$.

In conclusion, Charlotte's speed in $\dfrac{\text{m}}{\text{s}}$ is:

$\dfrac{5}{2}\,\dfrac{\text{m}}{\text{s}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Uche pumps gasoline at a rate of $18\,\dfrac{\text{L}}{\text{min}}$.

**What is Uche's pumping rate in $\dfrac{\text{mL}}{\text{s}}$?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\dfrac{\text{mL}}{\text{s}}$
hints:
We will convert $18\,\dfrac{\text{L}}{\text{min}}$ to a rate in $\dfrac{\text{mL}}{\text{s}}$ using the following conversion rates:

- There are $1000\text{ mL}$ per $1\text{ L}$.
- There is $1\text{ min}$ per $60\text{ s}$.

In conclusion, Uche's rate in $\dfrac{\text{mL}}{\text{s}}$ is:

$300\,\dfrac{\text{mL}}{\text{s}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Lúcia is using energy at a rate of $420$ kilocalories $(\text{kcal})$ per hour $(\text{h})$ ice skating. 

**At what rate is Lúcia using energy in calories per minute $\left(\dfrac{\text{cal}}{\text{min}}\right)$?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\dfrac{\text{cal}}{\text{min}}$
hints:
We will convert $420\,\dfrac{\text{kcal}}{\text{h}}$ to a rate in $\dfrac{\text{cal}}{\text{min}}$ using the following conversion rates:

- There are $60\text{ min}$ per $1\text{ h}$.
- There are $1000\text{ cal}$ per $1\text{ kcal}$.

In conclusion, Lúcia is using energy in $\dfrac{\text{cal}}{\text{min}}$ at a rate of:

$7000\,\dfrac{\text{cal}}{\text{min}}$

answers.message: This is the number of kilocalories (often called Calories with a capital C) per minute, but we need the number of calories (called calories with a lowercase c) per minute.

assessment_items.question_content:
A certain food has a gluten ratio of $13\,\text{mg}$ of gluten per $\text{L}$ of the food.

**What is the gluten ratio in micrograms per milliliter $\left(\dfrac{\mu\text{g}}{\text{mL}}\right)$?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\dfrac{\mu\text{g}}{\text{mL}}$
hints:
We will convert $13\,\dfrac{\text{mg}}{\text{L}}$ to a rate in $\dfrac{\mu\text{g}}{\text{mL}}$ using the following conversion rates:

- There are $1000\,\mu\text{g}$ per $1\text{ mg}$.
- There are $1000\text{ mL}$ per $1\text{ L}$.

In conclusion, the gluten ratio in $\dfrac{\mu\text{g}}{\text{mL}}$ is:

$13\,\dfrac{\mu\text{g}}{\text{mL}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Mia paints interior walls at a rate of $12\dfrac{\text{m}^2}{\text{h}}$.

**At what rate does Mia paint in $\dfrac{\text{cm}^2}{\text{min}}$?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\dfrac{\text{cm}^2}{\text{min}}$
hints:
We will convert $12\,\dfrac{\text{m}^2}{\text{h}}$ to a rate in $\dfrac{\text{cm}^2}{\text{min}}$ using the following conversion rates:

- There are $60\text{ min}$ per $1\text{ h}$.
- There are $100\text{ cm}$ per $1\text{ m}$. Therefore there are $(100\text{ cm})^2=10{,}000\text{ cm}^2$ per $1\text{ m}^2$.

In conclusion, Mia paints at a rate in $\dfrac{\text{cm}^2}{\text{min}}$ of:

$2000\,\dfrac{\text{cm}^2}{\text{min}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
A sound wave travels through iron at a rate of $5120\,\dfrac{\text{m}}{\text{s}}$.

**At what rate does the sound wave travel in $\dfrac{\text{km}}{\text{h}}$?**

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_sound]: (source)

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\dfrac{\text{km}}{\text{h}}$
hints:
We will convert $5120\,\dfrac{\text{m}}{\text{s}}$ to a rate in $\dfrac{\text{km}}{\text{h}}$ using the following conversion rates:

- There are $1000\text{ m}$ per $1\text{ km}$.
- There is $1\text{ h}$ per $3600\text{ s}$.

In conclusion, the rate in $\dfrac{\text{km}}{\text{h}}$ at which the sound wave travels through iron is:

$18{,}432\,\dfrac{\text{km}}{\text{h}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
The inclination (tilt) of an amusement park ride is accelerating at a rate of $2160\,\dfrac{\text{degrees}}{\text{min}^2}$.

**What is the ride's acceleration rate in $\dfrac{\text{degrees}}{\text{s}^2}$?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\dfrac{\text{degrees}}{\text{s}^2}$
hints:
We will convert $2160\,\dfrac{\text{degrees}}{\text{min}^2}$ to an acceleration rate in $\dfrac{\text{degrees}}{\text{s}^2}$ using the following conversion rate:

- There are $60\text{ s}$ per $1\text{ min}$. Therefore there are $(60\text{ s})^2=3600\text{ s}^2$ per $1\text{ min}^2$.

In conclusion, the acceleration rate in $\dfrac{\text{degrees}}{\text{s}^2}$ is:

$\dfrac{3}{5}\,\dfrac{\text{degrees}}{\text{s}^2}$

assessment_items.question_content:
A sheet of glass has a density of $2.5 \dfrac{\text{g}}{\text{cm}^3}$.

**What is the density of the glass in $\dfrac{\text{kg}}{\text{m}^3}$?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\dfrac{\text{kg}}{\text{m}^3}$
hints:
We will convert $2.5 \dfrac{\text{g}}{\text{cm}^3}$ to a density in $\dfrac{\text{kg}}{\text{m}^3}$ using the following conversion rates:

- There are $1000\text{ g}$ per $1\text{ kg}$.
- There are $100\,\text{cm}$ per $1\text{ m}$. Therefore, there are $(100\text{ cm})^3=1{,}000{,}000\text{ cm}^3$ per $1\text{ m}^3$.

In conclusion, the density of the glass in $\dfrac{\text{kg}}{\text{m}^3}$ is:

$2500\,\dfrac{\text{kg}}{\text{m}^3}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Riya is applying mulch to her garden. She applies it at a rate of $250{,}000\text{ cm}^3$ of mulch for every $\text{ m}^2$ of garden space.

**At what rate is Riya applying mulch in $\dfrac{\text{m}^3}{\text{m}^2}$?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\dfrac{\text{m}^3}{\text{m}^2}$
hints:
We will convert $250{,}000\,\dfrac{\text{cm}^3}{\text{m}^2}$ to a rate in $\dfrac{\text{m}^3}{\text{m}^2}$ using the following conversion rates:

- There are $100\text{ cm}$ per $1\text{ m}$. Therefore, there are $(100\text{ cm})^3=1{,}000{,}000\text{ cm}^3$ per $1\text{ m}^3$.

In conclusion, the rate in $\dfrac{\text{m}^3}{\text{m}^2}$ at which Riya is applying mulch is:

$0.25\,\dfrac{\text{m}^3}{\text{m}^2}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Lily's car has a fuel efficiency of $8$ liters per $100$ kilometers.

**What is the fuel efficiency of Lily's car in kilometers per liter?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\dfrac{\text{km}}{\text{L}}$
hints:
Note that we need to reverse the independent and dependent variables for this ratio and that we want a unit rate.

Since Lily's car has a fuel efficiency of $8$ liters per $100$ kilometers, we can likewise say it has a fuel efficiency of $100\,\text{km}$ per $8\,\text{L}$.

In conclusion, the fuel efficiency of Lily's car in $\dfrac{\text{km}}{\text{L}}$ is:

$12.5\,\dfrac{\text{km}}{\text{L}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Bulan rows on a crew team. Her team rows their boat at a split (rate) of $\dfrac{2\text{ min}}{500\text{ m}}$.

**What is Bulan's team's rowing rate in $\dfrac{\text{m}}{\text{min}}$ ?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\dfrac{\text{m}}{\text{min}}$
hints:
Note that we need to reverse the independent and dependent variables for this ratio and that we want a unit rate.

If Bulan's team takes $2\text{ min}$ to row $500\text{ m}$, we can also say they row $500\text{ m}$ in $2\text{ min}$.

In conclusion, Bulan's team's rowing rate in $\dfrac{\text{m}}{\text{min}}$ is:

$250\,\dfrac{\text{m}}{\text{min}}$
